Empires & Puzzles Bonecrusher Review: 5-Star Legendary Fire Red from Jungle Hunter Family

Bonecrusher was once the hardest hitting sniper in the game, capable of deleting enemies in a single fast-mana swing. But with modern stats leaving him looking like a glass cannon, is he still viable? We break down the math behind his massive multiplier.
Bonecrusher Review: The Silverback Who Smashes Skulls... If He Lives
Bonecrusher: The Glass Gorilla of the Jungle

Bonecrusher, the "Silverback Chieftain," burst into the game in early 2023 as part of the Jungle Hunter family. At the time, he was a terrifying force of nature—a Fast mana sniper who could arguably hit harder than anyone else in the game, provided his team was still standing. Fast forward to 2026, and while his damage multipliers are still eye-watering, his stats have aged like... well, like a banana left in the sun too long. He is a classic 5-star Legendary Fire hero who offers pure, unadulterated violence with very little utility. He doesn't cleanse, he doesn't dispel (mostly), he just hits you with a very large stick.

Let's be honest, this is a giant gorilla in red plate armor holding a spiky club. It’s not subtle. The art captures his brute force perfectly—he looks like he’s about to flatten a tank. The "Silverback" theme is strong, though some players might find the humanoid armor on a primate a bit uncanny. He’s certainly distinct from the endless parade of elves and knights; he’s a monkey with a mace, and sometimes that’s all you need.
Here is the hard truth: Bonecrusher is suffering from severe stat atrophy.

His base attack (912) was once god-tier. Now, modern heroes launch with 1300+ base attack. Even Limit Broken twice (LB2), he barely scratches 1125 attack, which is significantly lower than a modern hero's

With ~840 defense and ~1500 health, he is incredibly fragile by today's standards. Modern attackers often sport 2200+ HP out of the box. Bonecrusher is a true "Glass Cannon"—he can dish it out, but if a modern hero so much as sneezes in his direction, he might fall over.
Special Skill: Fury of the Silverback
This skill is the definition of "Win More."
540% damage at Fast speed is solid, but standard for a sniper.
is massive. In a full team (5 alive), that’s an extra 120% (4 allies x 30%), bringing his total modifier to

If your team is dead, he is dead weight. If he is the last man standing, he only does the base 540%. He is strongest at the start of the match and gets progressively weaker as the battle turns against you.
The +15% attack stack against weaker elements (Ice) is a nice bonus for Titan hits, but in PvP, you rarely hit the same target 10 times with a sniper.
Let's look at the damage potential versus modern creep.
Fast (8 tiles). With a lvl 29 mana troop or a bard bonus, he charges in 7 tiles.
Even with his massive conditional multiplier, Bonecrusher barely keeps pace with the
damage of a modern sniper, and falls behind top-tier ones. And that is
if his whole team is alive. If two allies are dead, his damage plummets well below modern standards.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Jungle Hunter family is a bit niche, but the passives do some heavy lifting.
The HP bonus (up to +20%) is nice if you run a full family team, but Jungle Hunters are rare to see together in modern defenses. It helps patch his low health stat slightly.
helps bridge the stat gap. Since it stacks with other buffs, you can pair him with an attack buffer (like Boldtusk or a modern bard) for huge tile damage.
The DoT (150 dmg) is negligible in today's meta of 3000+ HP heroes. It's mostly there to annoy or perhaps finish off a hero clinging to 1 HP.
is the best possible option for him. It simply makes his big hit bigger. Simple, effective, and exactly what he needs.
Upon release, Bonecrusher was often compared to a "Red Kageburado" or a superior Lianna. He was the premier deletion button for red stacks.

He has been crept hard. Not only are his stats low, but the mechanics of "sniper damage" have fallen out of favor compared to "Hit-All" or "Hit-3" heroes who also apply devastating ailments (Mindless Attack, Wither, etc.).
. He is no longer a defense candidate. On offense, he can still serve as a designated executioner in 3-2 or 4-1 stacks, especially if you protect him. He remains a top-tier choice for
against Green Titans due to his high attack multiplier, self-buff, and Barbarian bleed.
Bonecrusher has only one job: Kill the target. His emblem path should reflect that.
Maximizes his kill pressure. Every point of attack scales with his 660% multiplier.
. Trying to make him tanky is a lost cause; his base stats are too low to ever make him durable by 2026 standards. Your only defense is killing the enemy before they kill you.
At the start of a match (when everyone is alive), his 660% hit is one of the hardest single strikes in the game.
High attack stat (for his era), Barbarian class for Bleed, and a passive that increases attack against weaker elements make him a staple for Green Titans.
He buffs his own attack, meaning he doesn't strictly rely on a buffer to do damage.
His defense and HP are critically low. He dies to incidental damage.
He gets weaker as you lose. If you are in a 2v5 clutch situation, he loses a massive chunk of his damage potential, making him a terrible "last stand" hero.
He offers zero team support. No cleanse, no dispel, no heal. He is purely damage.
His best home. The stack against weaker elements and barbarian bleed are perfect here.
Great for taking out a specific threat in a cleanup team.
His self-buff counts towards the bonus, and his fast speed allows him to leverage the tournament rules well.
Defense down + Elemental Defense Down + Bonecrusher's hit = Dead enemy.
Maximizing tile damage and special skill damage against Green Titans.
If he misses his one big hit, he has contributed nothing to the fight.
He is a sniper. A Taunt hero like Krampus or Ludwig neutralizes him completely.
He kills one target. A reviver (Mother North, Alberich) undoes his entire contribution with one skill.

If you pull him early, he is a godsend. He will carry you through map stages and lower-tier raids by simply deleting bosses. Level him, but be aware he will eventually become too fragile for Diamond arena.
He is likely a bench-warmer for wars or a dedicated Titan specialist. Do
use Alpha Aethers (LB2) on him unless you are chasing high scores on Mythic Titans. There are far better modern reds (Constructs, Goblins, Cartoons) that deserve the resources.
"This hero seems like a red Kage. Great at the start of a fight. Terrible at the end of one."
- This early forum prediction turned out to be 100% accurate.
"King of Snipers, Hands down. That damage potential in fast is insane lol"
- A sentiment from 2023 that has aged poorly as stats ballooned.
"I pulled this crazy ape... LB2 destroyed the new wave of snipers."
- Players correctly identified that while he hit hard, the introduction of massive stat boosts (LB2) to
heroes hurt his ability to one-shot.

Bonecrusher is a relic of a simpler time when hitting someone really, really hard was a viable strategy. In the complex, ailment-heavy meta of 2026, he is a blunt instrument in a surgical theater. He is still satisfying to use when the stars align—watching a health bar simply disappear never gets old—but don't expect him to survive a counter-punch. He’s great for Titans, fun for farming, but retired from serious PvP duty.
A Titan specialist and mid-tier war attacker. Keep him for the big numbers on the scoreboard, but leave him at home for the war defense.
Is Bonecrusher still the king of the jungle in 2026? We analyze his massive damage potential, his fragile stats, and whether this fast sniper deserves your rings today.
